prioritized_issues.issue: N+1 HTTP requests — sequential fetch inside loop
prioritized_issues.explanation: For each user, two sequential HTTP requests are made inside the loop. With N users, this makes 2N requests instead of 2 (batch) requests, massively increasing network energy and latency.
prioritized_issues.optimization: Batch all user requests and all orders requests in parallel using Promise.all. Restructure the API to accept batch endpoints if available.
prioritized_issues.estimated_savings: Could reduce network energy by ~80% and wall-clock latency by ~80%

prioritized_issues.explanation: fs.readFileSync blocks the event loop during I/O. While the I/O itself is necessary, the sync call prevents other operations from executing concurrently.
prioritized_issues.optimization: Use fs.promises.readFile for async non-blocking I/O, or cache the config after first read.
prioritized_issues.estimated_savings: Could reduce CPU idle waste by ~40% during I/O wait

prioritized_issues.issue: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(obj)) deep clone — double serialization overhead
prioritized_issues.explanation: Serializing to JSON and parsing back is the most expensive way to deep-clone an object. It consumes CPU for serialization/parsing and memory for the intermediate string (2x the object size).
prioritized_issues.optimization: Use structuredClone(obj) (available in Node 17+ and modern browsers) which is a native, more efficient deep clone.
prioritized_issues.estimated_savings: Could reduce CPU energy by ~60% and memory by ~50% for large objects

prioritized_issues.explanation: Using += for string concatenation in a loop creates intermediate string objects, causing O(n²) memory allocations.
prioritized_issues.optimization: Collect message parts in an array and use .join('') at the end.
prioritized_issues.estimated_savings: Could reduce memory allocation energy by ~60% for large arrays

prioritized_issues.issue: File written on every batch call without append check
prioritized_issues.explanation: Each call to logBatch overwrites the log file. If called frequently, this causes unnecessary write amplification.
prioritized_issues.optimization: Use fs.appendFileSync or fs.promises.appendFile, or buffer logs and flush periodically.
prioritized_issues.estimated_savings: Could reduce disk I/O energy by ~30% if logBatch is called multiple times

recommendations: Replace sequential fetch loop in getUsersWithOrders with Promise.all for parallel requests
Replace fs.readFileSync with fs.promises.readFile in loadConfig
Replace string concatenation with array join in logBatch
Use fs.appendFileSync instead of fs.writeFileSync in logBatch to preserve logs
